signals.text: Brand went LIVE on FERMAT funnels around Apr 10-11 and is seeing strong early traction: 564 sessions on Apr 12 alone with 16 purchases and $965 revenue. 7-day CVR is 2.42% which is excellent for a new brand.

Very active Slack channel with 20+ messages in the last 14 days. Brand contacts (Matt Kagan, Jacob Popp) are highly engaged, sharing ad creatives, fonts, and providing detailed funnel feedback with screenshots.
Weekly check-in cadence established (every Tuesday 7:30 PM IST). Next call scheduled Apr 14. KOC completed Mar 31 with clear goals: increase Meta spend via optimized video funnels.
Brand spending ~$150,000/mo on Meta ads with $30 CAC and $72+ LTV. Goal is to INCREASE spend (not reduce CAC), which aligns perfectly with FERMAT value prop. Willing to tolerate up to $75 CAC.
Brand is only ~2 weeks old (created Mar 30). Still in onboarding phase with design alignment ongoing. Matt was offline Apr 7-10 for Passover, causing brief delays in funnel review.
Matt requested cart cross-sell fix on Apr 12 — wants to stop recommending shipping protection and actually cross-sell into other products. Needs follow-up resolution.
Rapid traffic ramp: 1 session on Apr 3 (testing) to 564 sessions on Apr 12. Revenue trajectory is strong at $1,496.25 total with 23 purchases across 3 active days.

recent.title: Grazly & FERMAT | Onboarding & Kick-Off
recent.summary: Grazly onboarding call. Matt Kagan (Co-Founder) handles all advertising. Company pivoted to beef brisket slabs as hero product (~88% of Meta spend). Goal: increase Meta ad spend via optimized video funnels (not reduce CAC). Current blended CAC $30, tolerable up to $75, LTV $72-120. All winning ads are voiceover-style videos. Plan: build 2 video funnels for beef brisket + bundle builder. Matt offline for Passover, Jacob Popp as backup. Go-live target: week 2. Carnivore Snax is direct competitor and also FERMAT client.

recent.title: Grazly | Intro Meeting
recent.summary: Sales intro call with Mukul. Matt heard about FERMAT on Twitter. Grazly on Shopify Plus, doing $1.1-1.2M/mo revenue, spending $150K/mo on Meta + $40K YouTube + $45K Amazon. No funnel currently — all ads route to homepage/PDP. Tried Replo but didn't work. Wants managed service ($2K/mo). No dev team. Interested in quiz funnels, bundle building, video funnels. Evaluating other CRO tools. Carnivore Snax mentioned as competitor also on FERMAT.

recent.title: Grazly <> Fermat (2024)
recent.summary: Initial outreach call from 2024 — Matt explored FERMAT but was not ready at that time. Company was earlier stage.

calls.analysis: Strong call cadence for a new brand: Intro call (Mar 24) -> KOC (Mar 31) -> Weekly Check-in (Apr 7, captured in Granola). 3 calls in 3 weeks with clear progression from sales to onboarding to optimization. Matt is highly engaged on calls, asking detailed product questions. Key insight: Matt wants to 'go hard on video funnels' and FERMAT should prioritize this. The Apr 7 weekly check-in revealed design alignment issues (fonts, colors, badges not matching website) which were addressed. Brand is now live and generating conversions as of Apr 10.

upcoming.note: Next weekly check-in. Should review live performance data (Apr 10-13 traffic).

upcoming.title: Grazly & FERMAT | Weekly Checkin
upcoming.note: Week 4 check-in. Should have 10+ days of live data for analysis.

messages.text: hey guys, how do i get the cart to stop recommending the shipping protection to people and actually cross sell into other items

messages.text: things look good. Im gonna work on getting this up and running

messages.author: Jacob Popp
messages.text: Aside from those 3 changes, I don't see anything else on my end. When Matt gets back tonight he can verify.

messages.author: Jacob Popp
messages.text: For this section please only include the meat snack videos. I think it creates disconnect when there are tallow videos on this lander.

messages.text: Can we please capitalize what is needed in this review. The first words of the sentences, 'i', and the name please.

messages.author: Pulkit Srivastava
messages.text: Hey Matt - great connecting today! Here's a quick recap from our kick-off call. Key Takeaways: Focus on Video Funnels, Beef Brisket Hero Product, Meta Spend Optimization, External A/B Testing. Action items listed for both Grazly and FERMAT.

messages.author: Mukul Varshney
messages.text: Hey Matt Kagan welcome to FERMAT! Introducing Pulkit as your Account Manager & Primary POC. Book your Kick-Off Strategy Call.

slack.analysis: Extremely active Slack channel for a 2-week-old brand. 40+ messages exchanged since Mar 30. Brand contacts (Matt Kagan + Jacob Popp) are highly responsive, providing winning ad videos, b-roll content folders, custom fonts (Bernier, Hore, Bebas Neue), and detailed design feedback with screenshots. Pulkit is responsive to all feedback. Key pattern: Jacob Popp handles creative/design reviews while Matt Kagan handles strategy and final sign-off. Most recent message (Apr 12) is Matt asking about cart cross-sell configuration — needs follow-up. The channel shows a healthy build-review-iterate cycle typical of successful onboardings.

email.analysis: Email cadence is solid for onboarding phase. Pulkit sent the KOC recap email on Mar 31 (same day as call), followed by onboarding resources email and a nurture email on Apr 2. Most emails are from Pulkit to the brand (outbound), with automated Glyphic summaries and calendar acceptances as inbound. No direct reply emails from Matt visible in search — communication is primarily happening on Slack, which is expected for an active engaged brand. The Apr 7 Glyphic summary confirms the weekly check-in happened. No concerning email gaps given the heavy Slack activity.
